title: Rankett.com — partner-program pivot + free-work clients
html:
TL;DR

  • Rankett's positional shift from "SaaS you sign up for" → "private partner program you apply for" — pull the signup button + pricing page entirely, replace with "Apply to Partner."
  • Three free-work clients are seeded as live simulations: rankett itself, scott thorson at Total Performance Sports Medicine, and Caspian at Merit Commercial Real Estate (tier-1 simulation includes building him an agency dashboard).
  • Fulfillment trichotomized: WP Plugin (60–65% of portfolio) / Antenna for DNS-compatible non-WP (20%) / Manual for locked Wix-Squarespace platforms (15–20%).
  • Pricing reset under one-time-optimization rework: $300 / $750 / $1,500 monthly tiers + separate one-time optimization priced as page-count × wholesale rate.
  • Landing-page copy hard-pivots from "AI Visibility, Your Brand, Our Backend" → "We handle the AI search layer. Your SEO team handles the rest" — explicitly carving out ai search optimization as additive, not replacement, to traditional seo.

The partner-program pivot

The big positional shift: stop being a SaaS, start being a private network. Cold email → application page → setter calls → "you qualified, first month free" → implementation call → paying partner. Auto-approval gate: average client must be $500/mo+ AND agency must have 5+ clients (the 60 day activation rule cousin — qualify the agency, not the volume).

Mechanics:

  • $299/mo platform fee to be a Rankett partner
  • First month free, earned through application funnel only — not publicly available
  • Fulfillment tiers ($498 / $998 / $1,498) layered as per-client add-ons on top of the platform fee
  • Slack channel for application notifications, Trello for lead tracking
  • ~$10/mo SMS, $50/mo setter subscription

Yahir's framing: "You're not a SaaS tool — you're a private infrastructure network for agencies that qualify." The psychological frame matters as much as the price.

Free-work clients

Three live simulation clients to harden the playbook before scaled rollout:

  1. rankett itself — eat-own-dogfood
  2. scott thorson at total performance sports medicine (totalperformance.clinic) — has SEO Manager WP access via elijah@rankett.com
  3. Caspian at merit commercial real estate (merit-commercial.com) — tier-1 simulation; deliverable includes shipping his website-guy a tracking/approval dashboard

The Caspian work doubles as a ghost white label proof-of-concept: a real client's web team gets an interface, can't see Rankett's brand.

The tri-modal fulfillment

The product splits cleanly by CMS-accessibility:

  • WP Plugin for wordpress sites (the bulk, ~60–65%)
  • Antenna for non-WP platforms with DNS access (~20%)
  • Manual for wix / squarespace / locked-down builders (~15–20%)

Yahir notes: change the ZIP file name from whatever it is now to "Rankett Plugin" — the small ops detail.

Pricing reset

Inside Yahir's "For YG" personal-todo block, he plans:

  • Ditch the pitch deck → white-labeled offer package agencies can use
  • Convert positioning guide into step-by-step + FAQ
  • Differentiate one-time optimization vs. monthly retainer
  • Page-count × wholesale rate for one-time pricing

New monthly tiers:

  • Tier 1: $300/mo (4 blogs + dashboard)
  • Tier 2: $750/mo (8 blogs + YouTube scripts + citations)
  • Tier 3: $1,500/mo (12 blogs + aggressive brand mentions)

Subtracted from monthly retainers (now in one-time optimization): schema markup for ai, FAQ structure, llm txt, blog optimizations. Monthly stays for ongoing maintenance + content velocity + brand-mention work.

Landing-page copy shifts

Old: "AI Visibility, Your Brand, Our Backend" / "Be the solution today. Do none of the work."
New: "We handle the AI search layer. Your SEO team handles the rest" / "We're not an SEO replacement. Your SEO team handles traditional rankings. Rankett handles what AI answers when someone asks ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Gemini for a recommendation."

The pivot: stop fighting seo, position as additive layer specifically for chatgpt / perplexity / gemini answer-surfaces.

Privacy

Doc contained a temporary password for the SEO-Manager WordPress login on totalperformance.clinic — redacted from this summary. Should be rotated by client.
